findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 40. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure that lists are structured correctly
findings.evidence:
Fix all of the following:
  List element has direct children that are not allowed: figure [<ol type="I">]; Fix all of the following:
  List element has direct children that are not allowed: figure [<ol type="A">]

findings.description: Repeated header/footer blocks inserted between page-equivalent content sections in the HTML, disrupting reading flow.
findings.evidence: In the continuous HTML document, the page footer ('State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries...') and the full letterhead header ('ROY COOPER Governor, ELIZABETH S. BISER Secretary, KATHY B. RAWLS Director, PT-2-2024') plus 'PROCLAMATION' heading are repeated between page 1→2 and page 2→3 content sections. A reader scrolling through the HTML encounters footer → letterhead → title block between each section of substantive content, which does not exist in the PDF's continuous reading flow.

findings.description: Two full-width images (likely page footer/header graphics from PDF page breaks) are inserted mid-content, breaking the flow of the main ordered list.
findings.evidence: First image (alt="", width 611.8pt) is inserted inside the Section VI (Onslow County) ordered list between item C (Queens Creek) and item D (Banks Channel). Second image (alt="", width 611.8pt) is inserted between Section IX (Brunswick County, item E) and Section X (General Information). These correspond to the PDF page break positions between pages 1–2 and pages 2–3, and appear to be page footer decorative graphics repeated mid-content.
